scenes.title: How Remotion Works
scenes.narration: Remotion lets you build videos programmatically using React — every frame is a React component.

scenes.title: What is Remotion?
scenes.narration: Remotion is a framework for creating videos in React. You write components, and Remotion renders them frame by frame.

scenes.title: Core Concepts
scenes.narration: Three concepts power every Remotion video: compositions, sequences, and interpolation.

bullets: Composition — defines width, height, fps, and duration
Sequence — places content at a specific frame offset
interpolate() — maps frame numbers to animated values

scenes.title: A Basic Component
scenes.narration: Every layout is just a React component that reads the current frame and animates accordingly.

scenes.title: Render Pipeline
scenes.narration: From React component to MP4 — here is how Remotion renders your video.

scenes.title: Before vs After
scenes.narration: Compare the old way of making videos to the programmatic approach.

scenes.title: By the Numbers
scenes.narration: The numbers behind Remotion's growing ecosystem.

scenes.title: What the Community Says
scenes.narration: Developers love how Remotion brings software engineering discipline to video production.

layoutProps.quote: Remotion is what happens when you let engineers build video tools.

scenes.title: Adoption Over Time
scenes.narration: Downloads have grown consistently as more teams adopt programmatic video.

scenes.title: Get Started
scenes.narration: Try Blog2Video to turn any blog post into a polished video automatically.
